week_01: 1. Sugar Land Skeeters | Dominant 9-1 week, balanced offense and pitching, multiple contributors firing | up
2. BaseballPro77 | Convincing 8-2 win, Mike Trout and Murakami both launched three home runs, clear power threat | up
3. General Hospital | Tied but posted elite team OBP (.414), 23 RBI, and 3 QS — talent is real even without a win | same
4. North Correa | Chris Sale looked dominant with 15 K and 2 QS, offense churned out 23 runs in a 7-3 win | up
5. Benchwarmers | Zach Neto was electric with 8 runs and 2 HR; 7-3 win despite modest pitching | up
6. Athletic Park Dragons | Gutted out a 5-4 win; Gleyber Torres and 4 NSVH were the difference-makers | same
7. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | Tied despite 7 HR and solid pitching — Imanaga's 7.00 ERA cost them dearly | down
8. Albuquerque Bombers | Won on pitching alone; Harrison was elite but the offense was paper-thin | same
9. Hiroshima Radiation | Nolan McLean's 15 K was a bright spot but fell 5-4; adding Colin Rea is a smart patch | down
10. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | Real offensive upside with Alvarez and Aranda but pitching is a major problem | down
11. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Lost 3-7 but have pitching pieces; Bryan Woo was solid and Soto adds floor | same
12. Buttville Skidmarks | Chase DeLauter's 4 HR is exciting but team ERA of 6.02 is unsustainable | same
13. Canberra Wombats | Dylan Cease looked sharp but Aaron Nola was a disaster; lost 2-8 with zero HR | down
14. Riverside Roughnecks | 1-9 beatdown, 7.91 ERA, zero QS — pitching is in crisis and must be addressed immediately | down
week_02: 1. Sugar Land Skeeters | Back-to-back elite weeks, 40 R this week is a season record, Drake Baldwin and Matt Olson both starred — this roster is the class of the league | up
2. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | 9–0 sweep with a 0.84 WHIP and 75 Ks is historically dominant; Ben Rice is a must-watch breakout — this team is ascending fast | up
3. Riverside Roughnecks | 7–3 win with 38 R, 10 HR, and a 2.42 ERA — this is the most complete performance of their young season and they look nothing like last week's pretenders | up
4. North Correa | Oneil Cruz went nuclear with 4 HR and 10 RBI, Ohtani chipped in — 7–3 result with elite power and solid pitching makes this a legitimate contender | up
5. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | Yordan Alvarez's .600 OBP is absurd and 77 Ks anchors elite pitching; the 6–4 win was workmanlike but this roster has genuine top-tier upside | same
6. General Hospital | A 2.10 ERA and .363 OBP losing 6–4 is a crime; the talent is real and the luck will turn — don't sleep on this team despite the ugly result | down
7. BaseballPro77 | Escaped with a 5–4 squeaker on the back of terrible ERA and only 2 HR — this team is living dangerously, but two weeks of wins is two weeks of wins | down
8. Hiroshima Radiation | Got destroyed 8–1 in a shocking reversal after looking strong; only 43 Ks and 1 category win is an organizational crisis that needs urgent attention | down
9. Athletic Park Dragons | Aaron Judge and James Wood are elite pieces but the pitching (4.30 ERA, 46 IP, 2 QS) is genuinely holding this team back from its ceiling | down
10. Buttville Skidmarks | Lost 7–3 despite decent pitching (3.19 ERA, 4 QS) — the offense simply isn't reliable enough to compete with top teams most weeks | same
11. Canberra Wombats | Lost 6–4 with real offensive contributors in Altuve (.577 OBP), Walker, and Henderson — they're not as bad as their record suggests but pitching keeps killing them | up
12. Albuquerque Bombers | Kyle Harrison was genuinely brilliant (1.65 ERA, 26 K) but lost 7–3 anyway; heavy waiver activity suggests the manager knows change is needed | same
13. Benchwarmers | A 5–4 win built on smoke and mirrors — 4.89 ERA, 42 Ks, and a historically lucky result; Corbin Carroll is a bright spot on an otherwise fragile roster | same
14. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | A 9–0 shutout loss with zero stolen bases and a league-worst 0 category wins is as bad as it gets; urgent roster reconstruction is needed | down
week_03: 1. Sugar Land Skeeters | Back-to-back elite weeks; 8 wins, 2.11 ERA, and an offense that scores from every position — nobody is touching this roster right now | same
2. Athletic Park Dragons | James Wood's .636 OBP and Contreras's .594 are not normal — 40 runs and .412 team OBP is a historic statement week; this team is ascending fast | up
3. BaseballPro77 | Jordan Walker's 5 HR explosion last week proved it wasn't a fluke; consistent top-5 category performer across all three weeks with solid pitching to match | same
4. Hiroshima Radiation | An 8–2 demolition of Canberra with 64 hits, league-leading 6 QS, and Bryce Harper's .552 OBP signals this team has fully woken up after a shaky Week 2 | up
5. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | A 1.65 ERA and 1.04 WHIP in a loss is the definition of unlucky — this pitching staff is elite and once the offense clicks, watch out | down
6. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | Strongest pitching in the league over three weeks may finally be enough to climb; offense remains a structural crisis that one good waiver week won't fix | up
7. North Correa | Chris Sale's 22 Ks and Oneil Cruz's 6 SB show the ceiling is enormous, but 51 IP and a 4.73 ERA in a losing effort reveals the floor is still dangerous | down
8. Albuquerque Bombers | Three active adds including Josh Jung and Noelvi Marte show a manager trying to fix real problems; another 5–4 loss stings but the lineup is improving | up

9. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | Merrill Kelly is a smart add and the pitching was creditable with 75 Ks, but an 8–2 blowout loss and zero NSVH points to a roster that needs more help than one wire move | same

10. General Hospital | The spotlight team this week — Kumar Rocker is a big swing and the 70 Ks in a tie show pitching upside, but a 5.48 ERA and a luck delta of -0.54 means they're genuinely underperforming expectations | down
11. Buttville Skidmarks | Elly De La Cruz is a weekly engine with 4 SB and 2 HR, but a 2.25 ERA somehow only produced a tie and the offense outside Elly remains thin | same
12. Riverside Roughnecks | Won 6–3 again with zero quality starts and a 1.43 WHIP — Trevor Story's 9 RBI carried them last week and this roster cannot keep winning on individual heroics alone | down
13. Canberra Wombats | Gunnar Henderson hit 5 HR and they still lost 8–2; this team has pop but the pitching is consistently too soft to compete in a full-category format | same
14. Benchwarmers | The luck index doesn't lie — a +0.91 luck delta masks a roster with serious pitching problems; 34 Ks and a 5.53 ERA will eventually catch up to them | same
